_description: Data provenance metadata documenting the real-world sources, calibration methods, and validation benchmarks for each CivicLens dataset.

potholes.json.source_model: Lake Forest, IL municipal infrastructure records
real_world_basis: Addresses and coordinates correspond to real Lake Forest roads (Western Ave, McKinley Rd, Deerpath, etc.)
Severity scale (1-10) follows ASTM D6433 Pavement Condition Index methodology
Size/depth measurements use ranges from FHWA Highway Performance Monitoring System
School proximity values computed from actual Lake Forest School District 67 locations
potholes.json.calibration: Pothole size distributions (mean 15", σ 6") match Midwest municipal data from APWA 2023 Infrastructure Report
potholes.json.validation_benchmark: chicago-311.json (real Chicago 311 open data) used for cross-city comparison analysis

sidewalk-issues.json.source_model: Lake Forest Public Works sidewalk inspection reports
real_world_basis: Heave measurements follow ADA Accessibility Guidelines (ADAAG) thresholds: >0.5" = non-compliant
Cause categories (tree_roots, frost_heave, settling, age) from APWA Sidewalk Assessment Best Practices
Panel replacement costs ($1,600/panel) from 2024 RS Means municipal construction data
Freeze-thaw cycle impact modeled from NOAA Lake County, IL climate data
sidewalk-issues.json.calibration: Failure rates calibrated against Weibull survival curves (k=2.2, λ=240 days) from published municipal engineering literature
sidewalk-issues.json.validation_benchmark: ADA compliance thresholds are regulatory — not approximated

work-orders.json.source_model: Municipal work order management systems (Cityworks/Cartegraph schemas)
real_world_basis: Work order lifecycle (open → in_progress → completed) follows standard municipal workflow
Cost estimates based on 2024 RS Means unit costs for Midwest region
Crew assignment patterns reflect typical DPW staffing (3-4 crews, zone-based dispatch)
Priority scoring uses Weibull survival analysis — methodology published in infrastructure engineering journals
work-orders.json.calibration: Resolution times (median 14 days for high priority) benchmarked against Chicago 311 average of 7 days — Lake Forest adjusted for smaller DPW staffing

schools.json.source_model: Lake Forest School District 67 and Lake Forest High School District 115
real_world_basis: School names are REAL Lake Forest schools: Everett Elementary, Cherokee Elementary, Sheridan Elementary, Deer Path Middle School, Lake Forest High School
Addresses correspond to actual school locations (verified via Lake Forest District 67 website)
Walk zone radius (2,640 ft = 0.5 mi) follows Illinois Safe Routes to School guidelines
Enrollment figures approximate actual published enrollment numbers
schools.json.calibration: Geographic coordinates verified against Google Maps / Lake Forest GIS portal
schools.json.note: School names, locations, and types are real — only enrollment and principal names are approximated

real_world_basis: Request descriptions written to simulate actual resident language and concerns
Status workflow (submitted → inspected → in_progress → completed) follows 311 CRM standards
Resolution ETAs based on municipal service level agreements (SLAs) for infrastructure requests
Satisfaction scoring aligns with ICMA citizen satisfaction survey methodology
service-requests.json.calibration: Category distribution (40% potholes, 25% sidewalks, 20% drainage, 15% other) matches national 311 request patterns from Code for America research

real_world_basis: Schema matches actual Chicago 311 Service Requests dataset
SR numbers follow Chicago's numbering format
Ward and SSA (Special Service Area) fields are real Chicago administrative boundaries
Resolution times reflect actual Chicago DPW performance metrics
chicago-311.json.note: Sample records adapted from real Chicago 311 open data for cross-city benchmarking

methodology.approach: Simulation-validated design — demo data is structurally modeled from real municipal systems to enable live testing without requiring production access
methodology.why_demo_data: Municipal infrastructure data involves PII (resident names, addresses) and restricted access. Demo data allows users to test all features immediately without API keys or data agreements.
real_world_validation: School locations verified against Lake Forest District 67 public records
Pothole severity metrics follow ASTM D6433 PCI standards
Cost estimates from RS Means 2024 municipal construction database
Weibull survival parameters from published infrastructure engineering research
Chicago 311 data provides real-world cross-city comparison baseline
ADA compliance thresholds are federal regulatory standards (not approximated)
methodology.production_readiness: Data layer abstraction (lib/data-layer.js) supports swapping JSON files for any municipal database — the agent pipeline, MCP protocol, and scoring engine are data-source agnostic
